growth_records.medium: BG11_0 (BG11 without nitrogen salts) + 2.5 g/L NaHCO3, diazotrophic (N-fixing)
growth_records.cite: Barbera, Elena et al. Continuous Cultivation as a Method to Assess the Maximum Specific Growth Rate of Photosynthetic Organisms. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00274. [PMC6811504]
growth_records.snippet: Table 4: Light 190 umol/m2/s, tau 1.32 d, cx_exp 0.47 g/L, mu_max 7.49 /d. 'mu = D = 1/tau'; steady state held >=5 d; diazotrophic BG11_0.
growth_records.notes: Continuous flat-panel photobioreactor (350 mL, CSTR), steady state maintained >=5 d. mu = D = 1/tau (paper eq.1). tau=1.32 d => D=0.7576 /d = 0.03157 /h; doubling time = ln2/D = 21.96 h. Steady-state biomass cx=0.47 g/L (dry weight, filtration/105C; Table 4). Incident light 190 umol photons/m2/s (PAR); 24+/-1 C; pH 7.5-8 (2.5 g/L NaHCO3 buffer). Volumetric biomass productivity Px = cx/tau = 0.47/1.32 = 0.356 g/L/d (paper eq.3). Photoautotrophic, diazotrophic in BG11_0 (no N salts); C from 5% CO2-enriched air sparged 1 L/h; energy from light. Table-4 'mu_max'=7.49 /d is a MODEL-derived ideal value (back-calculated via Haldane light + CTMI temperature + Monod N kinetic model, eq.19), NOT the actual growth rate; actual experimental growth rate is D=1/tau. Average model mu_max across all steady states = 8.22 +/- 0.69 /d. Autotrophic growth stoichiometry (elemental analysis): 0.2518 H2O + 0.0202 N2 + 0.2314 CO2 + phosphates -> C0.2314H0.5036O0.2210N0.0404P0.0036 biomass + 0.2513 O2 + 0.0054 OH-. Per-condition CO2 uptake / O2 evolution rates were NOT tabulated (O2 rates only from separate respirometric tests), so uptake/secretion left empty rather than invented. BG11_0 full salt composition given only in Supplementary (not in text) -> medium_composition_stated false.
